meta.purpose: Prioritized queue of health books to process via Gemini extraction into TrainAI knowledge base. Use the extraction prompt in GEMINI_EXTRACTION_PROMPT.md with Google AI Studio.
meta.workflow: 1) Open Google AI Studio, 2) Paste extraction prompt as System Instructions, 3) Upload ebook PDF or paste chapter text, 4) Save JSON output as knowledge/{slug}.json, 5) Add require() to api/coach.js and wire into system prompt

queue.title: Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity
queue.author: Dr. Peter Attia
queue.randy_relevance: HIGH — Attia's framework is directly structured around Randy's goals. VO2 max, grip strength, and eccentric strength are the three highest predictors of longevity and physical independence. His 'Centenarian Decathlon' concept: what do you need to physically do at 80? Work backward and train for it NOW. Randy's 50-mile mountain hiking trek is a concrete version of this.
key_topics_to_extract: Zone 2 cardio — definition, target heart rate, why it's the highest-ROI longevity lever. Randy's cycling and hiking qualify.
VO2 max — how to measure, target ranges by age, how to improve. Key longevity predictor.
Eccentric strength training — why the lowering phase matters, Nordic hamstring curls, single-leg lowering, relevance to hiking descents.
Grip strength — testing protocol, age-adjusted norms, tracking as longevity proxy.
Centenarian Decathlon framework — working backward from physical capacity targets at 80.
ApoB and cardiovascular metabolic risk (relevant to Randy's triglycerides and glucose flags).
Insulin resistance — Attia's detailed framework, very relevant to glucose 129 flag.
Sleep architecture and deep sleep — Attia's view on sleep as the master recovery lever.
Zone 5 / high-intensity intervals — when to use, how to combine with Zone 2.
randy_caveats: Randy's goal is to GAIN weight (147→160 lb), not lose it — filter fat-loss framing accordingly.
Kidney function (eGFR 51) means some aggressive protein targets may need contextualization.
Attia sometimes recommends creatine — Randy has stopped this due to elevated creatinine. Flag.
queue.coach_integration_target: Physical performance section: validate Zone 2 sessions, track VO2 max proxy via Oura trends, coach on eccentric strength programming

queue.title: Training for the Uphill Athlete
queue.author: Steve House, Scott Johnston, Kilian Jornet
queue.randy_relevance: CRITICAL for 50-mile trek goal — this is the definitive methodology book for multi-sport mountain athletes who mix trail running, hiking, ski mountaineering, and climbing in a single training year. Steve House is one of the world's best mountaineers, Scott Johnston was an Olympic-level cross-country ski coach, and Kilian Jornet is arguably the best endurance mountain athlete alive. The training principles are scientifically backed and athlete-tested. Directly applicable to structuring Randy's annual training plan around the 50-mile trek.
key_topics_to_extract: Block periodization for mountain endurance athletes — how to structure training phases leading to a major objective (directly applicable to 50-mile trek).
Zone 2 and aerobic base building methodology — how much, when, and how to know it's working. Complements Attia's Zone 2 framework with mountain-sport application.
Strength-to-weight ratio for uphill performance — how to build functional strength that aids rather than hinders endurance.
Specific strength exercises for mountain athletes — not gym bodybuilding, but movement-specific strength (single-leg work, hip stability, core for steep terrain).
Training week structure — how to balance gym days, Zone 2 sessions, and higher-intensity work.
Physical tests for mountain athletes — fitness benchmarks, aerobic threshold self-tests.
Recovery principles — how to build volume without overtraining, especially relevant given Randy's high weekly output.
Tapering protocol — how to peak for a major multi-day objective like a 50-mile trek.
Multi-day effort preparation — how the body responds to consecutive days of high-output mountain activity, nutrition and recovery strategies.
Altitude considerations if relevant to the trek location.
randy_caveats: Randy's 50-mile trek is the primary training objective — extract everything through that lens.
Randy is also a gym/weights athlete — the book's strength section may undervalue gym lifting. Extract principles, adapt to his hybrid program.
Randy needs to gain weight to 160 lb — any body weight reduction recommendations are not applicable.
High training volume recommendations should be contextualized against Randy's current 6+ hours/week — he may already be at or above base recommendations.
queue.coach_integration_target: Fitness section: trek preparation training blocks, Zone 2 volume tracking, strength programming for mountain performance, periodization guidance

queue.title: Brain Energy: A Revolutionary Breakthrough in Understanding Mental Health
queue.author: Dr. Christopher Palmer
queue.randy_relevance: HIGH — Palmer's thesis reframes mental health disorders (including depression) as mitochondrial energy disorders, not purely neurotransmitter imbalances. Directly relevant to the Wellbutrin context. Every lifestyle intervention Randy is doing (exercise, anti-inflammatory diet, sleep, gut health) is mitochondrial medicine. This is the mechanistic bridge between Randy's protocol and potential Wellbutrin reduction — but the framing must remain 'parallel support pathway' not 'replace medication.'
key_topics_to_extract: Mitochondrial dysfunction as root cause of depression and anxiety — mechanism chain.
How exercise specifically improves mitochondrial density and function — anti-depressant mechanism.
Metabolic psychiatry framework — dietary interventions for brain energy.
Ketogenic diet evidence for mental health (note: not fully compatible with Randy's high-carb performance fueling — extract mechanism, filter application).
Sleep and mitochondrial repair — why deep sleep is when mitochondria regenerate.
Inflammation → mitochondrial dysfunction → depression: the mechanism connecting gut health, autoimmunity, and mood.
Specific nutrients for mitochondrial function: CoQ10, B vitamins, magnesium, omega-3 (Randy already has fish oil).
randy_caveats: Palmer sometimes recommends ketogenic diet — not compatible with Randy's high-carb endurance fueling protocol. Extract mechanisms, not diet prescriptions.
NEVER use this book to suggest medication changes. Frame entirely as 'supporting the same targets through parallel pathways.'
queue.coach_integration_target: Sleep section: connect deep sleep to mitochondrial repair and mood. Supplement section: validate magnesium, omega-3 as mitochondrial support.

queue.title: Nature Wants Us to Be Fat
queue.author: Dr. Richard Johnson (nephrologist)
queue.randy_relevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — The 'fat' framing does NOT apply (Randy needs to gain weight, not lose it). However, Johnson is a nephrologist whose research is highly relevant to: (1) eGFR 51 / kidney inflammation, (2) fructose-uric acid pathway driving triglycerides and metabolic dysfunction, (3) the 'survival switch' mechanism causing mitochondrial downregulation and organ inflammation. Extract mechanisms, not the fat-loss prescriptions.
key_topics_to_extract: Fructose-uric acid 'survival switch' mechanism — how fructose triggers cellular energy downregulation.
Uric acid as inflammatory mediator — connection to kidney function, triglycerides, and metabolic dysfunction.
Kidney inflammation from metabolic dysfunction — very relevant to eGFR 51.
Mitochondrial dysfunction from sugar/fructose load — mechanism chain.
Dietary interventions for lowering uric acid and protecting kidney function.
Why high-fructose corn syrup and added sugars are different from whole-food carbohydrates (important for Randy's high-carb fueling strategy).
randy_caveats: Randy needs to GAIN weight — ignore all fat-loss prescriptions and caloric restriction advice.
Extract the organ-protection and metabolic mechanisms — not the weight loss framework.
Kidney function rules still apply: no creatine, monitor protein targets.
queue.coach_integration_target: Coach medical flags: glucose/triglyceride interpretation, kidney function context, uric acid awareness

queue.title: Built to Move: The Ten Essential Habits to Help You Move Freely and Live Fully
queue.author: Dr. Kelly Starrett and Juliet Starrett
queue.randy_relevance: HIGH for physical baseline testing — the 10 empirical physical tests function as a 'physical age' report card. The sit-and-rise test, balance tests, shoulder mobility, hip mobility are all trackable baselines. Given Randy's 50-mile hiking trek goal, the Starretts' connective tissue and joint health framework is directly applicable. He already uses Athlean-X and Moves Method for programming — this adds the assessment layer.
key_topics_to_extract: All 10 physical tests: exact protocols, scoring, and what each predicts.
Sit-and-rise test — scoring methodology, longevity correlation data.
Breathing mechanics — connection to HRV and nervous system recovery (syncs with Oura data).
Hip mobility protocols — directly relevant to hiking and squat mechanics.
Shoulder mobility and overhead position — important for Randy's lifting.
Connective tissue health — nutrition and recovery for tendons and ligaments.
Sleep position and recovery recommendations.
Walking targets and gait mechanics — relevant to hiking training.
randy_caveats: Randy has strong athletic baseline — test results context matters. A score that looks 'below average' for a sedentary person may be different for a hybrid athlete.
Some tests may need modification given current weight-gain phase.
queue.coach_integration_target: Log screen: physical baseline test tracker (quarterly check-ins). Fitness coaching: reference specific Starrett tests in mobility recommendations.

queue.title: The Pegan Diet
queue.author: Dr. Mark Hyman
queue.randy_relevance: HIGH — Hyman's Pegan framework (plant-rich + quality protein + anti-inflammatory fats) aligns closely with Randy's current eating patterns and health goals. Strong on autoimmune, inflammation, and metabolic health. Good bibliography for finding primary studies.
key_topics_to_extract: Autoimmune disease dietary interventions — TPOAb-relevant.
High-quality fat sources and anti-inflammatory nutrition.
Gluten and leaky gut mechanism (reinforces existing protocol).
Blood sugar regulation through food — relevant to glucose 129 flag.
Plant diversity framework.
Specific foods for hormone and thyroid support.
randy_caveats: Randy needs to GAIN weight — some of Hyman's caloric density guidance may need inversion.
GF already established — no need to re-extract that foundation.
queue.coach_integration_target: Nutrition coaching: food quality recommendations, anti-inflammatory meal building

queue.title: Fiber Fueled
queue.author: Dr. Will Bulsiewicz
queue.randy_relevance: HIGH — Gut-Hashimoto's axis, SCFA/butyrate mechanism, 30-plants-per-week target. Core to TPOAb reduction strategy. Already partially integrated in anti_inflammatory_protocols.json.
key_topics_to_extract: 30 plants per week framework — evidence base, how to count, practical implementation.
SCFA production and gut barrier integrity — full mechanism.
Specific prebiotic fibers and their microbial targets.
Fermented foods research (complements Stanford 2021 data already in knowledge base).
Autoimmune connections — any specific Hashimoto's or thyroid references.
High-fiber eating for athletic performance — any performance nutrition angle.
randy_caveats: Randy needs caloric density — high-fiber foods can be filling; frame as additions, not replacements.
queue.coach_integration_target: Nutrition section: fiber diversity tracking, gut health recommendations

queue.title: The Mind-Gut Connection
queue.author: Dr. Emeran Mayer
queue.randy_relevance: MEDIUM-HIGH — Gut-brain axis, 90% serotonin in gut, enteric nervous system. Reinforces the Wellbutrin parallel pathway argument. Scientifically rigorous — Mayer runs a gut-brain research lab at UCLA.
key_topics_to_extract: Vagus nerve and bidirectional gut-brain communication.
Gut microbiome → neurotransmitter production → mood.
Specific probiotic strains with mood evidence.
Stress → gut permeability → systemic inflammation pathway.
How exercise affects the gut-brain axis.
Any specific dietary interventions for depression via gut.
queue.coach_integration_target: Sleep/mood section: gut-brain axis framing, serotonin production context

queue.title: The Whole-Body Approach to Prostate Care
queue.author: Dr. Aaron Katz
queue.randy_relevance: MEDIUM — Prostate health through anti-inflammatory and lifestyle lens. Relevant given age 51, insulin resistance flag (glucose 129), and that PSA testing should likely start if not already done. Katz's framework overlaps heavily with anti-inflammatory work already in progress.
key_topics_to_extract: Insulin resistance → BPH (benign prostatic hyperplasia) mechanism.
Specific foods and supplements for prostate protection.
PSA monitoring recommendations.
Anti-inflammatory interventions for prostate health.
Hormonal balance and prostate — relevant given testosterone 531 ng/dL.
randy_caveats: De-prioritize — significant overlap with existing anti-inflammatory protocol.
Most valuable addition would be any prostate-specific lab markers to add to next labs list.
queue.coach_integration_target: Medical context: prostate health monitoring, PSA testing recommendation

processing_notes.gemini_model: Gemini 1.5 Pro or 2.0 Flash — both handle full book PDFs in one context window
processing_notes.prompt_location: Use the extraction prompt provided in your Claude session history
integration_steps: 2. Add const bookVar = require('../knowledge/{slug}.json'); to api/coach.js
3. Wire key insights into SYSTEM_PROMPT — brief, targeted section not full re-dump of JSON
processing_notes.priority_order_reasoning: Outlive (Attia) first because it covers VO2 max/Zone 2 which directly affects daily coaching output. Brain Energy (Palmer) second because Wellbutrin context needs the mitochondrial framing. Johnson third for kidney/metabolic mechanisms. Starretts fourth for physical baseline tests.
